commit.message:
fix: Telegram "no response generated" — synthesis, person fields, calendar range (#44)

Three root causes for empty/wrong Telegram responses:

1. When the LLM exhausted all 5 tool rounds, the synthesis round ran
   without tools AND without any instruction to produce text. The LLM
   saw a conversation ending in tool results and produced nothing.
   Fix: add explicit synthesis instruction + fallback from tool results.

2. person_info omitted birthday, company, and position fields — data
   from Apple Contacts was fetched but never shown to the LLM.

3. Calendar search hardcoded ±30 days, missing recurring events like
   birthdays months away. Widened default to ±180 days.

Also: gitignore live e2e test file (contains real names for integration
tests against live server).

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix game hang by correcting CPU branch timing

Branch taken (+1) and page-crossing (+2) cycle penalties were lost because
branch() incremented this.cycles but execute() overwrote it with the base
cost. Use a branchPenalty field that gets added after execute() returns.
Also remove temporary debug instrumentation from PPU and NesEmulator.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add reply_inbox fallback when private_replies fails

private_replies uses comment conv ID in URL (reverted from inbox conv ID
which caused 500). When private_replies fails (e.g., live video comments,
already replied), falls back to reply_inbox with inbox conversation ID
to send the message as a regular inbox message instead.

commit.message:
Remove -f/--file flag: agents read files themselves

File injection via -f dumped full file contents into agent prompts,
wasting context window on content the agent can access directly.
Agents have full filesystem access and should read files themselves.

Removed loadFiles(), formatPromptWithFiles(), FileContent interface,
and the glob dependency. Only --map (codebase map) remains as
injectable context, reserved for research/scoping agents.

commit.message:
Fix wrong chord names: musical root scoring + harmonic rolloff

Two causes of wrong chord names (Cmaj7→E7#9, Cm→Em9):

1. Wrong root selection: chroma energy sorts E above C because E sits
   in the mid-range where guitar resonates best, not because it is
   the bass note. Fix: score each detected note as potential root by
   how many other notes fall on musically expected intervals above it
   (5th=8pts, maj/min 3rd=6pts, maj/min 7th=4pts, etc.). C with
   G(5th)+E(maj3)+B(maj7) outscores E with G(min3)+B(5th)+C(min6).

2. Phantom major 3rd for minor chords: C's 5th harmonic lands on E,
   contaminating a Cm chord with an E that fights the real Eb. Fix:
   gentle high-frequency rolloff (weight = 1/(1+(freq-300)/500)) so
   bins at 650 Hz (where 5th harmonics of bass strings land) contribute
   ~50% vs 100% for fundamentals at 130-300 Hz. Eb (a real barre-chord
   fret at ~300 Hz) is almost unaffected; phantom E at ~650 Hz is halved.

Also raised STABLE_RATIO 0.50→0.60 to reduce harmonic phantom notes
(which appear in ~40-50% of frames) from reaching chord detection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Rewrite autoDetectCircuitLaps with distance-periodicity algorithm

The old algorithm used the finish GPS position as gate and simple proximity
detection — failing on XCO courses where start/finish can be 300-400m apart
and the compact trail crosses itself multiple times per lap.

New algorithm:
- Samples candidate gate points along the first 30% of the track
- Finds all proximity crossings (30m radius, 60s min gap)
- Tries 3 strategies per gate: all crossings, even-only, odd-only
  (handles courses where the trail passes the gate twice per lap)
- Scores by lap count / (1 + 5*CV) — favors consistent distances
- Detects start loop if first segment < 75% of full-lap median

Tested with real XCO race file (23.64km): correctly detects
1 Start Loop (2.83km) + 4 full laps (4.88km each, CV=0.0002)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add 18 new French regional classics (528 → 546 recipes)

New entries covering major missing regions and dishes:
- Cassoulet de Castelnaudary (Languedoc, 3-day white bean casserole)
- Baeckeoffe d'Alsace (3-meat Riesling casserole, sealed with luting paste)
- Tripes à la Mode de Caen (Norman 12-hour tripe in Calvados & cider)
- Potée Auvergnate (mountain salt pork & cabbage pot)
- Poulet au Riesling d'Alsace (chicken in Riesling with cream liaison)
- Mouclade Charentaise (Atlantic mussels, saffron, curry & Pineau)
- Waterzooi de Poulet à la Gantoise (Flemish-French aromatic cream broth)
- Ficelle Picarde (Picardy ham & mushroom crêpe gratin)
- Bourride de Lotte à la Sétoise (monkfish broth thickened with aïoli)
- Ttoro Basque (Saint-Jean-de-Luz fisherman's stew with Espelette)
- Socca Niçoise au Feu de Bois (Nice's chickpea flour galette)
- Sauce au Porto (classic port wine demi-glace sauce)
- Homard Rôti au Beurre de Corail (roasted lobster with coral butter)
- Mousse de Saumon Fumé en Gelée (cold salmon mousse in Champagne jelly)
- Chou Farci Braisé à la Bourgeoise (whole braised stuffed cabbage)
- Gratin de Cardons à la Moelle Lyonnais (Lyon Christmas cardoon gratin)
- Tarte Tatin au Calvados (original upside-down apple tart, Sologne)
- Crème Caramel Classique (the trembling bistro custard)

Also fix preview.html cache-busting: JSX now fetched with ?v=Date.now()

Sierpinski Bezier Web: 3D fractal with De Casteljau Bezier curves

Sierpinski tetrahedron fractal where every edge is a Bezier curve computed
using De Casteljau's recursive algorithm. Features custom GLSL shader for
stable per-depth coloring, tessellated curved faces, and full ImGui controls
for recursion depth, curve order, bulge, segments, scale, and per-depth colors.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: reliability, observability and GUI improvements (v6.2)

Reliability:
- live_trader: _refresh_state() error guard — keeps cached state on REST
  failure, logs REFRESH_STATE_FAILED event; on_closed_candle() outer
  try/except prevents WebSocket thread crash; WebSocket exponential
  backoff (min 5s x 2^attempt, cap 60s); gate released in finally block
- paper_trader: on_closed_candle() outer guard; 4-hour max-loss halt;
  signal drought detection with cooldown to prevent DB spam

Observability:
- constants.py: SIGNAL_DROUGHT_HOURS = 4.0, MAX_LOSS_PCT = None
- live_trader + paper_trader: drought event logged after 4h no raw signal
- trading_status.py: alert monitoring every 10s (drought banner, halt banner)

Performance:
- optimizer.py: ThreadPoolExecutor parallel trials (up to 6 workers)

Config & CLI:
- main.py: --max-loss PCT flag (4-hour halt when session PnL drops below
  threshold); _validate_config() checks leverage, symbols, intervals,
  days_back, trials at startup

GUI:
- Add Equity tab: reads balance_snapshots from SQLite, ASCII sparkline
- Re-opt countdown label in Best Strategy panel (updated every 2s)
- Band level (1-8) label in Last Signal panel
- Fix _load_trades() to query trades table from SQLite (was broken CSV)

Agents:
- market-analyst.md: signal verification + comparison vs last run sections
- trade-analyst.md: 4 new diagnostic queries (param drift, losing trade
  patterns, signal drought check, hourly performance by UTC hour)

Tests:
- tests/test_indicators.py: 18 tests (RMA, EMA, crossover, entry signals)
- tests/test_orders.py: 6 tests (apply_slippage)
- tests/test_backtester.py: 4 tests (smoke, flat market, wallet history,
  PnL consistency)
All 31 tests pass.

Docs: STRATEGY.md v6.2, README.md updated with all new features

Switch cache from time-based TTL to event-driven invalidation

Cache now persists indefinitely and only clears when content changes
(scraper publish, YouTube convert, admin actions). Between writes,
all traffic is served from memory — zero DB queries — letting Neon
CPU sleep until the next publish event.

commit.message:
Fix: Invert signal direction logic (calls vs puts were backwards)

Pattern detection was producing opposite signals 93% of the time.
Flipped all direction checks in fvg.py:
- Bearish gap now correctly produces CALLS (retrace up)
- Bullish gap now correctly produces PUTS (retrace down)
- Fixed C2/C3/C4/C5 color validation to match new directions

Results: Win rate jumped from 0.4% → 46%, avg P&L from -8.67% → +0.14%
- profit_lock exits: 91.2% WR (+8.96% avg)
- max_hold exits: 68.1% WR (+4.15% avg)
- stop_loss exits: 0% WR (-8.63% avg) ← still problem area
